findings.title: Worker overrides only ever applied to Workers[0]
findings.detail: applyWorkerOverrides unconditionally writes hostFlag/enabledFlag into out.Workers[0] regardless of how many workers exist in the config. If a config declares more than one worker, the --worker-host / --worker-enabled CLI overrides silently apply to only the first worker entry and leave all others untouched. There is no worker-selection flag and no error/warning, so an operator passing --worker-host to point a multi-worker deployment at a new box gets a partial, silent misconfiguration. It is also index-fragile: the semantics depend entirely on config ordering.
findings.suggested_fix: Either document/enforce a single-worker invariant (error if len(Workers)>1 and an override is passed), or add a --worker-name selector and apply overrides to the matched entry.

findings.title: Build gate re-runs on every push retry even when the tree is unchanged on the first attempt
findings.detail: The go build + go vet gate is inside the attempt loop. On attempt 1 the gate runs against the freshly cherry-picked tree; if the push then fails non-ff, the loop rebases and re-runs the gate. This is correct after a rebase (tree changed) but means the (potentially minutes-long) gate is paid again on every retry. More importantly, a rebase that drops all cherry-picked commits (e.g. they already landed on target) produces an empty result that still passes the gate and pushes a no-op, returning exit 0 as if promotion succeeded — masking that nothing was actually promoted.
findings.suggested_fix: Detect an empty/no-op rebase result (compare HEAD vs origin/target) and report it distinctly rather than reporting a successful promote; optionally only re-run the gate when the tree actually changed.

findings.title: Temp worktree cleanup swallows all errors, can leak registered worktrees
findings.detail: The deferred cleanup runs `git worktree remove --force` then os.RemoveAll, discarding both errors. If the cherry-pick/rebase left the worktree in a state git refuses to remove even with --force (e.g. lingering rebase-merge state), os.RemoveAll deletes the directory but the worktree stays registered in the main repo's .git/worktrees, accumulating stale administrative entries across repeated failed promotes. Nothing prunes these.
findings.suggested_fix: On cleanup failure, run `git worktree prune` as a fallback, or at least warn on stderr so the operator can prune manually.

findings.title: Unrecognised-verb error message omits valid verbs (reap, _shim)
findings.detail: The default-case error lists verbs 'start, stop, status, ps, attach, restart, logs, pause, resume' but omits 'reap', which is a real, dispatchable verb (case at line 61) and is advertised in superviseTopUsage. An operator who mistypes near reap gets a hint list that wrongly implies reap is not a verb. This is a hand-maintained duplicate of the switch that has already drifted.
findings.suggested_fix: Derive the verb list from a single source (a slice of verbs used both by the switch and the error message), or at minimum add 'reap'.

findings.title: Bare '--project=' (empty value) is not parsed and falls through to cwd silently
findings.detail: The `--project=` form is matched with `len(args[i]) > 10`, so an exactly-10-char arg '--project=' (empty value) fails the guard, is not recognized as a flag, and is silently ignored — the command then defaults to the working directory. A caller intending to force an empty/explicit project would instead get cwd config with no diagnostic. The hand-rolled slice-index parsing here also diverges from the flag-parsing style used elsewhere (promote uses strings.HasPrefix).
findings.suggested_fix: Use strings.HasPrefix(arg, "--project=") + strings.TrimPrefix like promote_cmd.go, and decide explicitly whether empty value is an error or means cwd.

findings.title: Bead-ID auto-detection error is fully swallowed, no diagnostic
findings.detail: When --bead is absent, the code runs `git log -1 --format=%s <sha>` to auto-detect a (hk-xxx) parenthetical; if subjectErr != nil the error is discarded and beadID stays "", so the Harmonik-Bead-ID trailer is silently not stamped and reconcile can never auto-close the bead (the exact stranded-in_progress failure the trailer exists to prevent, per the hk-53p3 comment). The operator gets no signal that auto-detection failed vs. genuinely found no bead.
findings.suggested_fix: On subjectErr, emit a stderr warning distinguishing 'could not read commit subject' from 'no bead ID present' so the operator can pass --bead explicitly.
